content.raw:
About Kris Krüg

Techartist, quasi-sage, cyberpunk anti-hero from the future.

My name is Kris Krüg. I am an AI keynote speaker, creative technologist, photographer, community builder, and culture hacker working at the messy edge where technology, creativity, ethics, and human weirdness collide.

The mission has not changed: humanize technology, fight apathy, democratize creative tools, and build communities with enough courage and imagination to shape what comes next.

Book me to speak See current work
AI speakerKeynotes, workshops, and executive briefings.
Community builderBC+AI, Vancouver AI, festivals, and field-building.
PhotographerConferences, movements, portraits, and impossible rooms.

What I am building now

These days my work orbits AI, community infrastructure, and creative practice. I help people make sense of machine intelligence without surrendering taste, culture, relationships, or responsibility.

I lead and support AI ecosystem work in British Columbia, give keynotes and workshops for creative and leadership audiences, build standalone learning portals around major talks, and help teams turn AI from vague pressure into useful practice.

BC + AI Punk Rock AI Both Hands Full

Start with the live projects

This is the current map: AI community infrastructure, keynote portals, and practical training resources that turn ideas into usable public artifacts.

Ecosystem

BC + AI Ecosystem

Community and industry infrastructure for a responsible and inclusive AI future in British Columbia.

Keynote portal

Both Hands Full

A keynote portal about keeping our souls intact while the machines get very good at making everything.

Creative rebellion

Punk Rock AI

Human agency, taste, and making culture with the tools without flattening ourselves into content machines.

AI adoption

Developing an AI Mindset

A practical starting point for teams and organizations learning to work with AI.

Find the right thread

This page is the biography. The deeper proof lives across a few focused pages, so search engines and humans can both follow the actual story.

WorkCurrent projects, portals, archive highlights, and active initiatives. SpeakingKeynotes, workshops, podcast guesting, hosting, and selected videos. ServicesAI training, strategy, creative technology, and practical team support. PublicationsThe legacy proof archive, cleaned up and linked from here. TestimonialsMore outside voices and social proof. Podcast EPKFor producers, hosts, and interview bookers.

The throughline

Humanize technology

Technology should resonate with the heartbeat of human experience, not bulldoze it. The point is not novelty. The point is agency, empathy, ethics, and imagination.

Fight apathy

I would rather be in the room than in the stands: challenging indifference, turning passive media consumption into active creation, and helping people participate.

Democratize art

Art is not a rarefied luxury. It is a common language. The best creative tools should help more people find their voice, not make everyone sound the same.

Build community

Communities are how ideas become durable. Convening is infrastructure for trust, courage, and collective action.

Photo proof, or how I got here

Before AI became the shiny object, I spent years with a camera in rooms where technology, activism, music, climate, celebrity, Indigenous leadership, startup culture, and internet history were all colliding.

The wild index

The old page had the right instinct: the giant lists are interesting because they show range. This version keeps the range, gives it shape, and makes the proof easier to scan.

Published, featured, or cited by
BBC NewsBC BusinessBoing BoingBoston GlobeBusiness InsiderCBCCNETDaily HiveDeSmogFast CompanyFinancial TimesForbesFortuneGeorgia StraightGlobe & MailHuffington PostLA TimesMashableMotherboardNational GeographicNational ObserverNew ScientistNPRQuartzReutersRolling StoneSlateTechCrunchTEDThe AtlanticThe Daily BeastThe GuardianThe NarwhalThe NationThe TyeeThe VergeThe WalrusThrasherTime MagazineToronto StarUSA TodayViceWall Street JournalWashington PostWIREDYale Environment 360

See the publications page for the deeper legacy list and source trail.

Portraits, interviews, and backstage moments
Barack ObamaBill ClintonDouglas CouplandEckhart TolleEvo MoralesGuy KawasakiHis Holiness the Dalai LamaIggy PopImogen HeapJack DorseyJad AbumradJohn LegendKim CattrallKrishna DasLaurence FishburneMalcolm GladwellMark ZuckerbergMichael StipeMikhail GorbachevNardwuarNeil YoungReggie WattsRichard BransonRodney MullenRoy Henry VickersShaquille O'NealStewart ButterfieldWayne GretzkyZoë Keating
Clients, collaborators, and rooms served
6S MarketingAccess to Media Education SocietyAmerican ExpressBC + AI EcosystemBurnkitCanada Wide MediaCanadian Broadcasting CorporationCIBCCity of VancouverConsulate General of the United StatesDalai Lama CenterDavid Suzuki FoundationDeSmogBlogDigiBCFuture in ReviewGetty ImagesHollyhockHootsuiteIEEEInternational Olympic CommitteeJohnson & JohnsonJones SodaLululemonMicrosoftNational Geographic PublishingNew Media BCPenguin BooksPentagramRed BullReel YouthSamsungSkeenaWildStrategic News ServiceTechvibesThe Firelight GroupTourism VancouverUNAIDSUnited Nations

The point is not name-dropping for its own sake. The range matters because it shows a career built inside real rooms: broadcast, activism, education, technology, culture, policy, festivals, and public imagination.

Things people bring me in for

AI keynote speaking

High-energy rooms that need clarity, courage, cultural literacy, and practical next steps.

AI mindset workshops

Hands-on sessions for teams learning to work with AI while protecting judgment and taste.

Community and ecosystem building

Convening, partner strategy, sponsorship, association building, and durable network infrastructure.

Creative technology strategy

Story systems, knowledge bases, archives, media, and tools that help organizations become more alive.

Want the useful door?

For current projects, start with Work. For keynotes, go to Speaking. For applied help with AI, creative strategy, or community systems, start with Services. For everything strange and specific, contact me directly.

Work Speaking Services Contact

excerpt.raw:
My name is Kris Krug – a boundary-pushing Creative Explorer, an intuitive Tech Whisperer, and a tenacious Culture Hacker. 

In the fabric of the digital era, where technology often overshadows humanity, I've taken it upon myself to chart a different course. I’m on a relentless mission to humanize technology, to infuse it with empathy, ethics, and emotion.
